title: Nikita Tsoy
text:
I am a fourth-year PhD student at INSAIT, supervised by Nikola Konstantinov. My research focuses on trustworthy machine learning, particularly on strategic incentives in machine learning systems and shortcut learning.
Before INSAIT, I received an MA in Economics from NES and a BS in Physics from MIPT.

Publications

Strategic Incentives

Tsoy, N., Kirev, I., Rahimiyazdi, N., & Konstantinov, N. (2025). On the Impact of Performative Risk Minimization for Binary Random Variables. ICML.
Chakarov, D., Tsoy, N., Minchev, K., & Konstantinov, N. (2024). Incentivizing Truthful Collaboration in Heterogeneous Federated Learning. OPT Workshop.
Tsoy, N., Mihalkova, A., Todorova, T. N., & Konstantinov, N. (2024). Provable Mutual Benefits from Federated Learning in Privacy-Sensitive Domains. AISTATS.
Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. (2023). Strategic Data Sharing between Competitors. NeurIPS.



Shortcut Learning

Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. (2025). On Measuring Localization of Shortcuts in Deep Networks. SCSL Workshop.
Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. (2024). Simplicity Bias of Two-Layer Networks beyond Linearly Separable Data. ICML.



Miscellaneous

Campbell, D., Brodeur, A., Dreber, A., Johannesson, M., Kopecky, J., Lusher, L., & Tsoy, N. (2024). The Robustness Reproducibility of the American Economic Review. I4R Discussion Paper Series.
